primary_concern: Sepsis risk with acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) and leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. Progressive AKI (Stage 1 to Stage 3) is evident with creatinine elevation. Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulopathy scoring. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and anemia (9.8 g/dL) suggest possible hepatic involvement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and AKI Stage 3 meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
Creatinine rise to 8.9 mg/dL (Stage 3) exceeds KDIGO criteria for AKI
Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulopathy scoring (threshold: <150 K/uL)
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor use not documented
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Perform fluid resuscitation and reassess perfusion status
Monitor lactate clearance and repeat within 2 hours
Order coagulation panel (INR, PT/TT) to assess bleeding risk
Consult nephrology for AKI management and RRT evaluation
Review excluded creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) for specimen integrity
doctor_handoff: Prioritize source control investigation for sepsis. Verify vasopressor requirements for septic shock confirmation. Review platelet trends for transfusion threshold (<50 K/uL).

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.

family_communication.english_summary: In the last 12 hours, your loved one has developed signs of a serious infection-related illness (sepsis risk confirmed). Their kidney function has worsened significantly (Stage 3 acute kidney injury), and blood tests show low platelets (88-92,000) and signs their liver may also be affected. The care team has started strong antibiotics, is giving fluids to support blood flow, and will check how their body is responding in 2 hours. They are also reviewing a very high kidney function test (8.9 mg/dL) to confirm if it was accurate. We are closely watching for any changes in blood pressure or bleeding risk.
family_communication.regional_summary: पिछले 12 घंटों में आपके प्रिय के शरीर में संक्रमण से जुड़ी गंभीर स्थिति (सेप्सिस) के लक्षण मिले हैं। उनके गुर्दे का काम करना बुरा हो गया है (तीसरे चरण की तीव्र गुर्दा समस्या), और खून के टेस्ट में प्लेटलेट्स कम (88-92,000) दिख रहे हैं और यह भी लगता है कि यकृत प्रभावित हो सकता है। चिकित्सा टीम ने शक्तिशाली एंटीबायोटिक शुरू कर दिए हैं, रक्त प्रवाह के समर्थन के लिए तरल पदार्थ दिए जा रहे हैं, और 2 घंटे में शरीर के प्रतिक्रिया की जांच की जाएगी। वे एक बहुत ऊँचे गुर्दा फंक्शन टेस्ट (8.9 मिग्रा/डीएल) की जांच कर रहे हैं ताकि यह पुष्टि की जा सके कि क्या यह सही है। हम रक्तचाप या रक्तस्राव के जोखिम में कोई बदलाव देख रहे हैं।
